message:
test(fabric): add brokerless rebalance E2E scenario with fence + repair verification

Add a complete brokerless rebalance test flow to the fabric E2E suite.
This is the 7th scenario in the aggregated report, exercising the
self-rebalance path where a cell autonomously decides to change its
steward set without a central broker.

Test flow:
1. Create a 6-candidate pool across 6 racks with mixed StorageClass
   (2 Durable, 4 Standard) and ascending latency (5-10ms)
2. Build a SubjectCell with epoch 11:2 and 3-target repair policy
3. Plan a rebalance based on ObservedCellLoad(256), yielding a Warm
   temperature with 3 stewards
4. Construct RebalanceCutEvidence with repair symbol bindings, consumer
   lease transfers, and reply right reissuance
5. Certify the self-rebalance, verifying the resulting cell's epoch
   advances to generation 3
6. Verify the old sequencer lease is fenced (authoritative_append
   returns StaleSequencerLease with the new holder and generation)
7. Capture shared control shard assignment

Assertions verify: temperature=Warm, 3 stewards, generation=3,
fence_generation matches, 4 repair holders, shard assigned, old
sequencer fenced, timeline captured.

Determinism test: run_brokerless_rebalance(same_seed) produces
identical BrokerlessRebalanceOutcome.

Helper additions:
- rebalance_candidate(): StewardCandidate with configurable storage
  class and latency
- brokerless_rebalance_policy/candidates(): shared test fixtures
- brokerless_repair_bindings(): computes repair symbol holder set
  based on temperature-dependent witness targets

Aggregated report updated: 7 tests, 7 passed, >=39 assertions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
